From my perspective, the game was okay and felt like an improvement over the previous title. It was slightly more stimulating as it utilizes concepts like trajectory optimization and precise timing.

Regarding what didn't work well: the visual boundaries of the darkness are very poorly defined. If even a single pixel of the player's hitbox touches the dark zone, it registers as a failure, which feels unfair and frustrating. Furthermore, the core gameplay is somewhat bland, and the moving lights have patterns that are too simplistic to convey a genuine sense of danger.

However, the final switch mechanic worked quite well. It was effectively guarded by a large light halo. Although the halo's movement was cyclic and predictable, it required strict timing to access the switch without being devoured by the absolute darkness.
It was really fun, the game loop was very enjoyable and made me want to improve on the next go. My ability to control my character was very stable and really smooth.
